THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ON VERDICT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT DEFENDANT MERIJOHN DDS, GEORGE K. P.C. MERIJOHN DDS, GEORGE K. RECOVER FROM PLAINTIFF PANAITE DDS, DOINA DR. DEFENDANT'S GEORGE K. MERIJOHN, D.D.S.P.C. AND GEORGE K. MERIJOHN, D.D.S. ARE ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF, DOINA PANAITE, D.D.S. IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT SAID PLAINTIFF, DOINA PANAITE, D.D.S SHALL RECOVER NOTHING BY REASON OF HER COMPLAINT, AND COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES SHALL BE COSIDERED BY THE COURT PURSUANT TO NOTICED MOTION. Mtn To Compel And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF DOINA PANAITE, D.D.S. MOTION TO COMPEL; AND FOR SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Steven J. Garrett, Esq. of O'Connor and Associates 693-9960 for pltf & moving party; Jennifer M. Ellis, Esq. of The Goldman Law Firm 435-5500 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court granted the motion to compel. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

Ntc And Motion To Compel; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN J. GARRETT OF O'CONNOR AND ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 693-9960 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JENNIFER M. ELLIS OF THE GOLDMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 435-5500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.
RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED AND CLARIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF DOINA PANAITE'S Motion To Compel And For Sanctions is Granted in part. As to Request For Production of Document 7, Defendants are ordered to produce all documents responsive to these requests reflective of communications concerning Defendants' future plans for practicing dentistry, the valuation of Defendants' former practice and the utilization of human cadaver tissue or animal source materials for periodontal treatment. Request No. 7 to include discussion of referrals & fiscal performance. As to Request For Production of Documents 23, 45 and 49, Defendants are ordered to produce all documents responsive to these requests to the extent that Defendants possess anything beyond that which is contained in the patients' charts the production of which would not violate the parties' stipulated order as to patient confidentiality. As to Request For Production of Documents 43, 65 and 69, Defendants are ordered to produce all documents responsive to those requests in Defendants' possession. The motion is otherwise denied. As to 43, 65 and 69 to include income from practice and dental consults. PERFORMANCE TO BE DONE BY SEPTEMEBR 8, 2011. As to Defendants' 30-page opposition brief, it violates CRC 3.1113(d) in that Defendants filed an oversized brief without obtaining prior relief of the court under CRC 3.1113(e). Defendants are directed to not do so again. MR. GARRETT TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported = (301/PJB)
